_note: Slab c.1320-1200 BC — the Hittite empire at its zenith and the Bronze Age climax, built around the MARQUEE CROSS-LINE BRIDGE: the Egyptian-Hittite peace marriage. Prefixes: Hittite=HIT_, Assyria=AS_, Kassite Babylon=BAB_. Extends the existing tails HIT_MURSILI2 (Hittite), EG_RAMESSES2 (Egypt), BAB_KURIGALZU2 (Kassite Babylon), referenced as strings and NOT recreated. THREAD 1 HITTITE (from HIT_MURSILI2): Mursili II's sons Muwatalli II (who fought Ramesses II at the Battle of Kadesh, 1274 BC) and Hattusili III (the younger son who would usurp the throne); Muwatalli II > Urhi-Teshub / Mursili III (his son, who succeeded then was DEPOSED by his uncle Hattusili III); Hattusili III (REAL son of Mursili II, uncle of the deposed Urhi-Teshub) & his powerful queen Puduhepa; their children Tudhaliya IV and the princess Maathorneferure; Tudhaliya IV > Arnuwanda III and his brother Suppiluliuma II, the LAST Hittite Great King, ~1200 BC, on the eve of the Bronze Age collapse. THREAD 2 THE PEACE MARRIAGE (the cross-line connection, Hittite x Egypt): Maathorneferure, daughter of Hattusili III & Puduhepa, was sent to Egypt and married Ramesses II in his regnal year 34 (~1245 BC), sealing the peace that had begun with the world's first known peace treaty (Hattusili III & Ramesses II, 1259 BC); a SECOND Hittite daughter was sent later in a further diplomatic marriage (noted, not drawn as a distinct node). THREAD 3 ASSYRIA note only: the Middle Assyrian chain (Adad-nirari I > Shalmaneser I > Tukulti-Ninurta I) is ALREADY complete in 10c_assyria.json, so nothing structural is added here to avoid double-parenting; the Assyria-Babylon link is by CONQUEST, not marriage: Tukulti-Ninurta I of Assyria defeated and captured Kashtiliash IV of Babylon ~1225 BC (carried in Kashtiliash IV's label, THREAD 4). THREAD 4 KASSITE BABYLON (from BAB_KURIGALZU2): Kurigalzu II > Nazi-Maruttash > Kadashman-Turgu > Kadashman-Enlil II (correspondent of Hattusili III of Hatti — a Babylon-Hittite diplomatic link) > Kudur-Enlil > Shagarakti-Shuriash > Kashtiliash IV, defeated & captured by Tukulti-Ninurta I of Assyria ~1225 BC. Note BAB_KADASHMANENLIL1 (Kadashman-Enlil I) already exists and is distinct from the new BAB_KADASHMANENLIL2 (Kadashman-Enlil II). Middle-chronology dates; every child year is smaller than its parent's. Web-verified against Wikipedia 'Muwatalli II', 'Mursili III', 'Hattusili III', 'Puduhepa', 'Tudhaliya IV', 'Suppiluliuma II', 'Maathorneferure', 'Egyptian-Hittite peace treaty', 'Nazi-Maruttash', 'Kadashman-Turgu', 'Kadashman-Enlil II', 'Kashtiliash IV', 'Tukulti-Ninurta I', 'Kassite dynasty'. No fabrication; uncertainty flagged where present.

families.note: HITTITE — the two sons of Mursili II (existing tail): Muwatalli II, the Kadesh king, and his younger brother Hattusili III, the future usurper.

children.label:
👑 Muwatalli II
c.1295 BC · Great King of the Hittites; son of Mursili II (REAL father-son); fought Ramesses II of Egypt to a standstill at the Battle of Kadesh, 1274 BC; moved the capital to Tarhuntassa · historical

👑 Hattusili III
c.1267 BC · Great King of the Hittites; younger son of Mursili II (REAL father-son); as viceroy he deposed his nephew Urhi-Teshub & usurped the throne; made the world's first known PEACE TREATY, with Ramesses II of Egypt, 1259 BC · historical

families.note: HITTITE — Muwatalli II to his son Urhi-Teshub (Mursili III), who succeeded him and was then deposed by his uncle Hattusili III.

children.label:
👑 Urhi-Teshub (Mursili III)
c.1272 BC · Great King of the Hittites; son of Muwatalli II (REAL father-son); succeeded his father but was DEPOSED after ~7 years by his uncle Hattusili III & exiled · historical

families.note: HITTITE — Hattusili III & his powerful queen Puduhepa (added here); their children Tudhaliya IV (heir) and the princess Maathorneferure (sent to Egypt — see the peace-marriage family below).

b.label:
👑 Puduhepa
c.1265 BC · Great Queen (Tawananna) of Hatti; daughter of a priest of Kizzuwatna; wife of Hattusili III & one of the most powerful women of the ancient Near East, co-sealing treaties & corresponding with Egypt in her own name · historical

children.label:
Maathorneferure
c.1260 BC · Hittite princess, daughter of Hattusili III & Puduhepa (REAL parents); sent to Egypt & married to Ramesses II (~1245 BC) as King's Great Wife, sealing the Egyptian-Hittite peace · historical

👑 Tudhaliya IV
c.1237 BC · Great King of the Hittites; son of Hattusili III & Puduhepa (REAL parents); built the Yazilikaya rock sanctuary; lost the Battle of Nihriya to Assyria's Tukulti-Ninurta I · historical

families.note: HITTITE — Tudhaliya IV to his two sons who reigned in turn: Arnuwanda III, then Suppiluliuma II, the LAST Hittite Great King before the Bronze Age collapse (~1200 BC).

children.label:
👑 Arnuwanda III
c.1215 BC · Great King of the Hittites; son of Tudhaliya IV (REAL father-son); reigned only briefly & died without an heir · historical

👑 Suppiluliuma II
c.1210 BC · the LAST attested Great King of the Hittites; son of Tudhaliya IV (REAL father-son); won the first known naval battle (off Cyprus) but presided over the fall of Hattusa in the Bronze Age collapse, ~1200 BC · historical

families.note: CROSS-HOUSE MARRIAGE (Hittite x Egypt) — THE MARQUEE PEACE MARRIAGE: Maathorneferure, daughter of Hattusili III & Puduhepa, married Ramesses II (existing) in his year 34, ~1245 BC, sealing the peace begun by the 1259 BC treaty. A second Hittite daughter was later sent in a further marriage (noted). Union only; no issue drawn.
KASSITE BABYLON — Kurigalzu II (existing tail) to his son Nazi-Maruttash, whose reign was the peak of the Kassite dynasty.

children.label:
👑 Nazi-Maruttash
c.1307 BC · King of Kassite Babylon; son of Kurigalzu II (REAL father-son); campaigned against Assyria & Elam; his reign marks the peak of the dynasty · historical

families.note: KASSITE BABYLON — Nazi-Maruttash to his son Kadashman-Turgu, correspondent of Hattusili III of Hatti.

children.label:
👑 Kadashman-Turgu
c.1281 BC · King of Kassite Babylon; son of Nazi-Maruttash (REAL father-son); ally of Hattusili III of Hatti against Egypt · historical

families.note: KASSITE BABYLON — Kadashman-Turgu to his son Kadashman-Enlil II (distinct from the earlier Kadashman-Enlil I already in the tree), who corresponded with Hattusili III of Hatti (a Babylon-Hittite diplomatic link).

children.label:
👑 Kadashman-Enlil II
c.1263 BC · King of Kassite Babylon; son of Kadashman-Turgu (REAL father-son); came to the throne young & exchanged the famous diplomatic letters with Hattusili III of Hatti (Babylon-Hittite link) · historical

families.note: KASSITE BABYLON — Kadashman-Enlil II to his son Kudur-Enlil.

children.label:
👑 Kudur-Enlil
c.1254 BC · King of Kassite Babylon; son of Kadashman-Enlil II (REAL father-son); a period of temple-building & economic activity · historical

families.note: KASSITE BABYLON — Kudur-Enlil to his son Shagarakti-Shuriash.

children.label:
👑 Shagarakti-Shuriash
c.1245 BC · King of Kassite Babylon; son of Kudur-Enlil (REAL father-son); his name means 'the Sun-god gives life' · historical

families.note: KASSITE BABYLON — Shagarakti-Shuriash to his son Kashtiliash IV, whose defeat by Assyria is the Assyria-Babylon link BY CONQUEST (no marriage; none fabricated).

children.label:
👑 Kashtiliash IV
c.1232 BC · King of Kassite Babylon; son of Shagarakti-Shuriash (REAL father-son); DEFEATED & captured by Tukulti-Ninurta I of Assyria ~1225 BC, who sacked Babylon & carried off the statue of Marduk (Assyria-Babylon link by conquest, not marriage) · historical
